caption:
If ONE MORE PERSON asks for my name…

Okay okay I’m showing off.
BUT ALSO let me share some inspiration.

There’s a HUGE difference between:

• Wanting something REALLY BADLY, and
• NEEDING something

And it’s what allowed me to build this body.

When I look in the mirror today, most of the time I burst out laughing. It’s just so ridiculous to me that I, of all people, somehow got jacked! Can you believe that?!

Yes, I wanted to.
Yes, I studied how to do it.
Yes, I’ve doubted every part of the training.
Yes, many times I think I look skinny and I should do better.
Yes, I started training again after I stopped for 6 months.
Yes, I train when I’m tired or sick.
Yes, sometimes I skip training for no reason.

I knew from the very start: my body is going to look the way it will.

I will do *the things*, and then… I’ll wait and see what happens.

The benefit is in the process and in my overall health anyways. I can’t lose.

When I didn’t become huge after 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 years…that’s just the game.

The only thing that matters is that you keep playing.

Keep dancing.

And keep enjoying how ridiculous life is.

Bonus points if you get the reference. Infinite potential:

Look around. Notice. Almost everything around you was created by people no…bigger than you.

Give your biggest gift to the world, even if it’s small.

Make your own dent in her universe.

Here’s to the crazy one. The misfits. The rebels. The troublemakers. The thick pegs in the shallow holes. They’re not fond of rules, and they have no respect for the status quo.

You miss 100% of the shots you don’t take.

The way to get started is to quit talking and begin taking.

Believe you can and you’re halfway there. Maybe that’s as far as you’ll get, but that’s okay.

#entrepreneurmindsets #motivationalquotes #entrepreneurshipmindset

It does really feel like waking up, as odd as that might sound.

#meditation #awareness #presence

Two years ago today we visited Iceland and gosh…so much beauty. Almost every 10 minutes we had to stop in awe.

What an incredible place.

Let’s go there again?

“Why no pictures of people?” Because they matter more.

The photos are the in-betweens.
The nooks and crannies of time.
The liminal.

But the magical moments, the sparkling smiles and deepest dances...are uncaptured.

Living in our shared memories—lingering in the consciousness that connects us all.

(Okay, maybe some of it is captured by my AI microphone 😉)

But that’s how we show our reverence to the people and moments that matter.

We give ourselves completely to the moment.
We allow it to slip out of our hands.
We live it fully, knowingly, intensely.

#mvu

Here’s what I think will matter in a post-AI world:

You.

I know it sounds like the opposite—aren’t we going to be replaced?

Well, I’m talking about a very specific you.

The capital-lettered YOU.

The YOU that’s an explosion of character, creativity and charm.

You see, between all the efficiency and perfection… we’ll want something REAL.

People queue to see the Mona Lisa every single day because something compels us to experience the real thing, the original, the One.

So when everything’s perfect and efficient, we’ll be yearning to connect with those who live from the heart.

We’ll be visiting that one bakery down the street because the person there is just so nice to talk to. We’ll walk in the cold to get our croissants because they’re wrapped in the warmth of someone we truly admire.

With every passing day it’s becoming more important that we become better, greater people that live more from their heart:

- Your imperfection makes you real and unique.
- We desperately want to connect with the Great People in our lives.
- And the AI is being trained on your interactions right now.

Let’s all become Great People as best as we can.

How to dress well

I was surprised by all your reactions so here we go: my principles for dressing well. I’ll mention my brands too.

➡️ PRINCIPLES

Clothing has three priorities:

1. HEALTH
2. FUNCTION
3. BEAUTY

Notice COMFORT is missing.
Sadly most of the world has comfort as #1.

Any comfort that takes away from health or function is a red flag.

No overly cushioned shoes: they weaken your balance and mess up your ankles.

No skinny jeans: you can’t sprint and stretch in it—basic human movement, we are animals meant to move.

No “plastic” fabric on the skin: it smells bad and is probably filling your balls with microplastics.

Of course you can have a lazy day in pajamas once in a while. But keep it at home, not in the supermarket or the airport.

Beauty matters.
A life filled with beauty is a better life.
This is not opinion, it’s biology.

We have evolved to appreciate and seek beauty.
Our sense of beauty is a proxy for “fitness” in the evolutionary sense.

This is why those three points are actually the same: beauty = health = function.

➡️ TACTICS

FIT comes first. Tailored clothes are the biggest different makers in all three principles.

My brands:
@suitsupply 
@sonofatailor 
@tailorstore 

FUNCTION second.

This means I mostly wear classic menswear shapes and colors. Grandpa pants, buttoned shirts, jackets. White, navy, grey, brown colors.

Boring, quick to build an outfit and everything looks good.

These high waisted pants were an experiment and I love it. They look great, they’re wool which is an amazing fabric, and they let me move freely without stretching, bunching or tearing.

FABRIC is third.

I love wool and linen. They have amazing properties for heating, cooling, moisture wicking. They look great. They don’t need complicated chemical processes and petroleum to be produced.

Tencel / Lyocell is great too. Doesn’t smell or absorb as much as cotton, eco friendly and super soft.

Cotton is a staple too, of course.

➡️ CONCLUSION

Dress well. It’s worth investing in tailored, classic, basic items in natural fabrics.

Makes everything better.

Anything you’d add?

New Instagram feature? As the world grows more and more connected, we feel more separate than ever.

To be frank, social media has done a terrible job.

On the surface, they help us "stay in touch" with our "friends."

But in reality, they just care about their bottom line.

- Apps are designed and optimized to make you spend more time inside the app.

- They play on our need for validation so we show off fake lives and get addicted to likes.

- They would rather show another Reel than have you talk to your friends—because you'll see more ads.

But...all the technology, all the networks...they're already here.

They know what you like.
They know where you are.
They know who you talk to.
They know when you have free time.

The only thing missing is a deliberate design decision.

It's so, so easy to nudge people to do things that'll make them happier.

So why aren't we doing it?
What if ChatGPT helped us with real-life connection?

There's so much hypocrisy in this "AI" hype. It turns off a lot of people who see the bullshit and think that's all. But it's not...

The real benefits are just unsexy.

Back when I worked at KPMG we were using AI all the time.

Not what you see today with video avatars or music generation.

But real serious stuff like recognizing fraudulent bank transactions.

Used to be a TON of manual labor.
Difficult.
Boring.

We replaced it with AI.

Or how we (high-end consultants) had to waste our precious time annotating PDF's with today's date.

I created a little automation for it that spread through the company like wildfire.

The point I want to make is this...

We don't need to solve our most difficult problems with the most advanced technology.

We just need to have a good, structured look at what you're doing.

95% of the time we can create small, simple systems that save you hours of time and many hairs of frustration each week.

So if you're running a successful business and you're trying to deal with a firehose of clients or programs or tasks...

Reach out. Maybe I can see something that can change everything for you.

And get you one step closer to what we all want in our business:

Calm.

A live webinar is a complex beast and you've got to have all the elements in place.
ㅤ
First things first: the countdown timer.
ㅤ
It's funny—these days people don't realize when something is actually live!
ㅤ
So put a timer on the page that counts down to the main event.
ㅤ
Webinars are so special exactly because they're live.
ㅤ
But don't stop there.
ㅤ
Don't put in the hard work only to have everyone forget about it.
ㅤ
Automated emails are your secret weapon.
ㅤ
Set up a series of reminders:
ㅤ
- 1 week before
- 1 day before
- 1 hour before
- 15 minutes before
- "We're live!"
ㅤ
And include a calendar invite so your viewer's digital calendar knows what's happening.
ㅤ
Now, the webinar's over. You crushed it. But wait...
ㅤ
The game's not over yet!
ㅤ
Enter: the follow-up sequence.
ㅤ
For those who missed out (or want to relive the magic), send them the replay.
ㅤ
Don't let the replay exist forever—otherwise your audience will procrastinate.
ㅤ
Just leave it for a day or two.
ㅤ
Now, I know what you're thinking.
ㅤ
"Marcel, that's a lot of moving parts!"
ㅤ
You're right. It is.
ㅤ
But trust me, it's worth it.
ㅤ
The difference between a good webinar and a great one?
ㅤ
It's all in the details.
ㅤ
Speaking of details...
ㅤ
Here's a pro tip: Use OBS Studio for your visuals.
ㅤ
It's like giving your webinar a Hollywood makeover.
ㅤ
Switch scenes, add overlays, and your viewers will think you're in a TV studio even if you're at home not wearing any pants.
ㅤ
It's super cool. I love it.
ㅤ
---
ㅤ
Oh one more thing:
ㅤ
Don't EVER let ANY of your content exist just ONCE.
ㅤ
Use your recording—it's great short-form content.
ㅤ
You can even use AI tools like Opus to do it automatically.
ㅤ
---
ㅤ
A great webinar campaign is like a well-oiled machine.
ㅤ
Each part plays its role.
ㅤ
Set it up right, and you'll be crushing it. With love, of course. 😉

Sunlight before screen light (it's not just about the light).
ㅤ
Anyone who has ever used a camera in manual mode knows:
ㅤ
Indoors it's DARK.
Even when it doesn't feel dark.
ㅤ
"Even when under shade, wearing a hat and sunglasses when outdoors, light levels are still at least 11 times higher than indoors with fluorescent or LED lighting."
ㅤ
So, given how recent indoor spaces are,
could it be that humans...aren't really adapted for this darkness?
ㅤ
If you're reading this you probably know this already. Of course!
ㅤ
So what's the other thing?
ㅤ
This morning I sent a message to my subconscious.
ㅤ
Because the first thing in the day was calm and peaceful—this set the tone.
ㅤ
You know, so often we set our alarm clocks so we have to rush to work and get there right on time (or a few minutes late).
ㅤ
That rush is going to stay with you for the rest of the day.
ㅤ
I'm sure you had a "slow morning" before and loved how you felt.
ㅤ
The thing is—you can do this even with a full working day ahead.
ㅤ
Decide to spend just 5 or 10 minutes being awake yet completely relaxed and you'll be surprised by how much of an impact this has.
ㅤ
You're telling yourself: it's okay. Everything is okay.
ㅤ
(Because it is! I'm imperfect, you're imperfect, we both fail all the time and have more to do than we'd wish. That's okay.)
ㅤ
My challenge to you: in the coming week, take a moment in the morning, however short it is, to be calm before you start the rush.
ㅤ
Let me know how it went.
ㅤ
Go crush it. With love. 🚀❤️

"I have no idea what I'm doing"
"Really? Wow, you learn quickly"
ㅤ
If there's one thing I've learned in corporate is that nobody really has all the answers.
ㅤ
It's not a bad thing.
ㅤ
As Ray Dalio says, "embrace reality and deal with it."
ㅤ
I see every single business as a raft that's out on the seas. It's a rough place and the raft is held together by a whole bunch of pieces.
ㅤ
We can never take apart the entire raft or we'll sink. The best thing we can do it take pieces out, look at them, and maybe replace them.
ㅤ
Even when we replace pieces of the raft, we're not sure if the new part is better.
ㅤ
That's why I like to look at business (and life) as a *set of hypotheses*.
ㅤ
- In marketing, "if we make posts talking about a topic that's interesting for our target customers, we'll have leads contacting us."
- In sales, "if we have a prospect meeting and do this specific presentation, they will purchase this product."
- In fitness, "if I go to the gym 4 times per week and do 4 sets of 15 reps, and I eat 150g protein per day, I'll gain muscle mass."
ㅤ
The day when we take inventory is the day when we can have a clear, honest look at what we're doing.
ㅤ
We can't reliably get better if we don't remember the decisions we made.
ㅤ
This is how you can organize the entire business and your entire life.
ㅤ
It starts with the overarching hypothesis:
ㅤ
"If I run my business/life according to these processes, habits and routines—it will be successful." (According to your definitions of "success," of course.)
ㅤ
Then you just break it down.
ㅤ
Business:
ㅤ
- Customer acquisition
- Delivery
- Finances
ㅤ
Life:
ㅤ
- Health
- Wealth
- Relationships
ㅤ
Then you go deeper and deeper.
ㅤ
When you go through your day with that "business hypothesis" or "life hypothesis," over time you'll have an excellent blueprint.
ㅤ
If you're an entrepreneur, you can take half of that business book and copy-paste it to the next business you start.
ㅤ
That's immensely valuable.
ㅤ
It's a level of being organized and strategic that is very rare.
ㅤ
But it's absolutely the fastest way to learn.
ㅤ
It's the way to learn so quickly that the people around you might not even realize you've been learning.

I have a superpower: I think sometimes.
ㅤ
Your energy is drained every day going through the motions?
ㅤ
Not sure if you're on the right path, in life?
ㅤ
Can't come up with good ideas?
ㅤ
Well...
ㅤ
That sucks, so let's go consume some social media 🙃
ㅤ
I mean—it's funny because it's true, right?
ㅤ
We got addicted.
ㅤ
Every time there's an unpleasant feeling:
ㅤ
- I don't know what to do here
- I got hurt emotionally
- I'm overwhelmed
ㅤ
...we scroll.
ㅤ
It immediately numbs the discomfort, and immediately blocks us from working through this challenge.
ㅤ
(This doesn't have to be big, I catch myself wanting to consume when I need to think 5 seconds about what to write here 😉)
ㅤ
When was the last time you just *stood there*?
ㅤ
Just standing there.
ㅤ
Nothing else.
ㅤ
I challenge you: do this today.
Pick a random moment, set a 10 minute timer, and just stand there.
ㅤ
The first 15 seconds will be fine.
Then you'll feel your hand go to your phone.
You'll have to resist that over and over.
ㅤ
And I pretty much guarantee that,
at the the end of those 10 minutes,
you'll have an insight.
ㅤ
Go crush it, with love. 🚀❤️

Do you agree? Even with AI, you have to learn to use your tools well. Because nothing can beat the human touch and your work will stand out if you can use your computer better.

This is one of the lessons I've had to learn the hard way.

You see, I'm very healthy. I only know a handful of people who pay as much attention to their food, fitness and sleep as I do.

I don't get sick.
My mind is super sharp.
My energy can run all day long.

So when I had to undergo surgery to get a brain tumor removed, I thought "yeah okay I'm super healthy so I'll be back in business after a week or two maybe."

It took three months.

Months where I spent the day sleeping in the couch and the night sleeping in my bed. My daily movement wasn't much more than moving between the couch and the bed.

Part of me felt fine. I wanted to move. I wanted to LIVE!

So when nobody was watching I'd do just 20 simple air squats...and have a massive headache for two days after (I had already maxed out the painkillers).

This was something I truly couldn't overcome.

No matter how healthy you are,
no matter how mentally tough you are,
sometimes all you can do is wait and hope.

Today I woke up feeling low energy. I thought it was just because I was teaching a class at 5AM but my Oura ring score was 35—I've only gone that low maybe three times in the four years I've had the ring.

So now I know: don't try to fight it.

My score was already trending down for a few days.

Today my body is asking me to stop.

So I press the big red "RECOVERY MODE" button in my mind.

Drop the commitments, get back to bed and hopefully tomorrow I'll have recovered.

Life's lessons start out quietly but if you don't listen, they will shout harder and harder until you finally hear them.

And the best way to recover is to just switch over to rest, completely, for a short period. Your body has incredible healing abilities so let it do what it does best.

Stay healthy, friends.

#lifestyle #personalgrowth #ai #health #ouraring #recovery #healthpractices #oura #fever #sick

That was not just some ink splattered on a slab of dead tree.

It *pulled* me in.

Suddenly I stopped worrying about how she would react to my text, I stopped brainstorming about what I could eat tonight, and I stopped hearing the techno that leaked through the headphones of the guy next to me. Everything was gone.

This is what beauty does to you.

Whether we realize or not, we're all looking for more beauty—but when it comes to creation it takes a backseat.

We buy Apple products because they're so beautiful—so we can create an online course with a shitty webcam that we upload straight to Loom.

We meticulously curate our focus playlists on Spotify—so we can smash out illegible emails or cookie-cutter AI-generated content in 3 seconds.

We travel to Bali so we can overlook the gorgeous nature while we're typing out the ugliest Google Doc the world has ever seen.

It's nuts.

Throughout history, humans have been captivated by beauty—whether in art, nature, or each other. But today it seems like the world is obsessed with the 80/20 rule, striving for Ultimate Efficiency at the expense of everything else.

Just look around you in any modern city. Plain, square, concrete slabs of buildings. Ornaments securely kept as relics of the past, to be admired but never reproduced. Even modern art eschews decades of hard work over paint splatters.

Only my friends from ex-USSR countries seem to notice that...this isn't really the way we want to live.

We want to live surrounded by beauty.

We want to experience being lifted up by beautiful stories, carried by beautiful music and we want to rest our eyes on beautiful places, humans and things.

As entrepreneurs, we are creators. We are actively shaping this world so I believe it's our responsibility to bring more beauty into it.

Put in more effort than the 20% and turn your work into something you're really proud of.

Learn the basics of aesthetics, graphic design and photography so every part of your business is *on point*.

Use your money to give back and support artists.

#BeautyMatters #Entrepreneurship #creative #Aesthetics #SoulfulLiving #creativity #art #artistlife #creator

The 3 Stages of AI Thinking:

Aaron went through these in the last 10 minutes. As you learn to use ChatGPT (and others) you'll move through the Stages too, multiple times, until AI Thinking is in your core.

How many messages have you exchanged with ChatGPT (or Claude or Bard, any Generative AI really) in the last week? Three, ten, 30?

So let's go through this thought process.

Stage 1: Doing it Myself.

"Hmm, I need to come up with 10 questions I could ask my audience for my product. Let's open a blank note page."

Stage 2: Opening ChatGPT.

"Oh wait, ChatGPT would actually be really good at this. Let me open it and start typing out what I want."

Stage 3: Talking to ChatGPT.

"Oh wait, I actually don't have to type it out. Let's hit the microphone icon, walk around a bit and just ramble about what I want."

Now you've become the curator.

That job will only become more and more valuable.

Dress up. Embrace the beauty of taking life seriously while finding joy in the little things. 

Our society often romanticizes childhood, admiring the innocence and simplicity that children embody. While it's true that children have a unique way of experiencing the world, we must also acknowledge the limitations that come with it. Children are not burdened with the weight of responsibility, yet they lack the agency and ability to create that adults possess.

Taking life seriously doesn't mean abandoning laughter and playfulness. On the contrary, it's about finding a balance that allows us to approach life with care and commitment.

Caring for ourselves is the foundation for caring for the world. When we make a genuine effort to prioritize self-care, such as getting enough sleep and maintaining our physical and mental well-being, something transformative happens. We demonstrate to ourselves that we value and care about even the smallest aspects of our lives. This fosters a deep sense of happiness and contentment within us, nurturing our inner child and allowing it to flourish.

Dressing well becomes an integral part of this process. It serves as a ritual of self-care, a daily reminder to consider the people we encounter and how we can bring beauty and positivity to their lives through our appearance. The concept of "enclothed cognition" suggests that our behavior is influenced by how we dress. By consciously choosing our attire, we set an intention for how we want to approach the day and interact with others. Dressing well is not merely a superficial act; it is a form of good manners, a way to show respect and build mutually positive relationships.

So, let us dress up and bring a bit more beauty into our lives. Let us appreciate the privilege of being able to take things seriously, knowing that it allows us to navigate life with thoughtfulness and purpose. And in our interactions, let us always strive to be warm, welcoming, and considerate, for it is through genuine connections with others that we find the most fulfillment in our lives.

Trust in the path.

Sometimes, the voice in my head can be actually helpful.

I remember sitting next to someone in a personal development seminar. We had just learned about healing your self-image and it was time for an exercise.

As instructed I looked them in the eyes and the speaker asked us to repeat some sentences:

"I see you."
"I see how hard you've been trying."
"You are a beautiful person."
"You are worthy of all the love you can get."

This stuff can be AWKWARD. Saying those deep words to a stranger? Weirddd

Cue the inner voice.

"What weird ass thing is this, Marcel. You know how much you paid to come here? How is just saying some words to a stranger going to suddenly fix all your mental problems?"

You recognize this, right? The usual stuff 😅

But then it changed...

"Hey bro, you're here. You've made the commitment to come, traveled across Europe and only going back in two weeks."

"You can choose: stay in doubt, or trust the teachers and give it your best shot. Just EVERYTHING you have. There's literally nothing you can lose from this. You can evaluate when you get home. Do this now with all the love you've got in you. Yeah, ALL of it."

"Make. It. Matter."

Zooming back out, tears started rolling from their eyes.

I'm sure you know this, that expression in someone's face when what you said, started loosening something deep inside of them.

We hugged as if we were the closest friends.

Afterwards, they told me that this moment was one of the defining ones in the whole event.

All of that just from the decision to go all in.

Joseph Campbell says it even more beautifully and I can't believe it took so many years for me to understand this.

Have you ever gotten the advice "just do what you love"? Gee, thanks but what the F am I supposed to do with it?

So what is meaningful?

It's when we FEEL like what we are doing, matters. In some grander scheme than ourselves.

So obviously good, deep connections with friends who are good at showing gratitude, is a key element.

But that's not the root.
Why are those friends showing gratitude?
Do they have better lives?

Nope.

You're going to hate me for it but I simplify things.

It's this (again): decide & remember.

1. Decide that meaning is something you want to feel.

2. Remember what you need to do to.

Meaning? It's about SEEING the meaning.

And the seeing happens in the observer, not in what's observed.

Ask yourself right now: what did you do that was meaningful today? Think hard! You need training. If you HAD to pick something, however small, what could it be?

- I tried to make someone smile today
- I worked out this morning
- I did one tiny thing that gets me closer to a dream

Here's Campbell's quote:

"Life has no meaning. Each of us has meaning and we bring it to life.
It is a waste to be asking the question if you are the answer."

Go crush it. With love.

When there is chaos to tame, lists come to the rescue. I call this: Just-In-Time Planning. Make a list right at the moment you need it.

Now the second secret is writing your thoughts right next to those tasks. Any information that might be useful when you're executing.

Because so often we (well I at least) have to figure out WHAT I want to do! So I don't know what the task list will look like until I've thought more.

Actually for planning this makes sense too.

You only really know how long it will take at the very last moment. This is why we plan our work in 2-week sprints instead of 5-year plans.

Late starts are a known optimization method of Critical Chain Project Management. I'm not making this up to be lazier :)

With digital work, you can:

- Add structure later. Start writing, reorganize your words when it's necessary.

- Add features later. This is the core of startups: make something that's already good enough and make it better.

- Add purpose later. We can just make stuff! It's okay to decide what you want to do with it later.

Want to learn more?

Make more.

Structure your work so that you produce Intermediate Packets, smaller blocks of work that can be reused and re-compiled into later work.

I'm doing the same thing for these posts. They start with me writing an article / video script, I make a long video on YouTube, and then I cut it into small pieces and extract quotes for other social media platform.

So often we forget, in all the hurry and the chase for achievement, how much of a core tenet it is for humans to connect with others.

Then we suddenly have one great interaction—it can even be just a few moments long—that lifts our spirit for the whole week.

I want to challenge you this week. When you're interacting with someone, try to really make it about just the interaction. You're there, there's nothing else that matters at this moment. Lift some spirits.

How? It's a deceptively simple framework:

Decide & Remember.

Decide that you will do this. Close your eyes and visualize what that will look like, how you would do it. Choose to want this.

Remember. Visualize now that moment you almost forgot, then suddenly remembered, then deeply connected. Maybe set an alarm on your phone. Just make sure you don't forget.

Hope you will enjoy this.

Go crush it. With love.

#personalgrowth #personalgrowthjourney #charisma #weeklychallenge #connect #heartcentered

It's one of the most wonderful feelings: to at some point stop and realize that hey, life is actually pretty great!

But we can't feel that if we're filled with distraction. It's kind of this last little thing that pops up once you let go of all other thoughts.

I want to encourage you to start training presence. It's simple but not easy. Luckily, you can't be "bad" at it. Just do it over and over and over again.

How? Every time you notice you're thinking something else, like "oh this thing on my desk I should do something with it" or "did I remember to feed my dog last night?"—

1. Notice and acknowledge the thought
2. Decide to not follow it
3. Return to where you were

It can be stressful if you're afraid you'd forget important things. So either trust that your subconscious will remind you another time, or just write it down.

Good luck. And most of all, enjoy.

I mean that very explicitly: literally make sure you say to yourself, hey let's enjoy this for a bit, and then do it.

♥️

Nobody can stay 100% focused 100% of the time. The key for more deep work is understanding this, going with your waves of attention and gently pushing them back to focus.
.
When you feel yourself drifting: breathe deeply, move around a bit, take a moment to look in the distance and think about nothing, or tell yourself "let's just focus for 15 more minutes."
.
This is not easy.
.
But you can train. Sitting in meditation and focusing on your breath is a core practice to increase your focus.
.

Here's an insight in the creative process. People ask me how they can take better pictures—so I say: the most important thing is to look *at* the picture and not *through* it.
.
Wait, what?
.
When you compose an image the subject is...the image. Not what's happening on the image. It's a rectangle with straight edges and round shapes and blobs of colors, not a team of colleagues on a buggy. It's a weird mental shift. 
.
Yes, I'm literally asking you to step away from "the moment" (oh no but mindfulness!!). And to step into the moment of creating a beautiful image. 
.
This is why sometimes I deliberately not bring my camera. 
.
It's also why painters find it notoriously difficult to paint the eyes in the (vertical) center of the head. 
.
Anyways, I love creating. This process of feeling, searching for why it feels this way, building towards the emotion I want to evoke. It's crazy how much happens in the split second before pressing the shutter. 
.
But you really have to focus on exactly what you want.
.
.
.

New year's resolutions are worth keeping. But they slip away too quickly.

Actually, why do we even wait until the start of a new year? 
How could it be if you had a blank slate every week instead? If every Sunday you had a chance to reconnect with your goals, to evaluate the past week and to start again with a new dose of zest?

The question above is one of my Sunday questions. They help me celebrate the past week, evaluate myself and set new intentions.

There's some pretty challenging questions in there, but they're worth the effort. Questions like * How much of a leader have I been last week?
* What can I start doing to increase my health next week?
* What did I struggle with last week? How can I honor this struggle?
* What do I need to do to be a better role model?

If you're interested in the full list, check out the article on the blog! Visit samyn.co/post/weekly-questions or click the link in my bio.

Technology shouldn't capture our attention, shouldn't be in our face and shouldn't try to do things better than us.

Technology should guard us like a bonfireâquietly crackling in the background, keeping us warm in the background while we connect and grow.

Because after all, humans are beautiful and our flourishing is what it's all about.
Technology quietly supports Humans feel alive most around people they care for or making things that matter. Not being dopamine-bombarded by consumption.

Technology is beautiful In a beautiful environment we can live in peace. Our sense of beauty is one of the most powerful ways to access the wisdom and processing of our subconscious.

"Vibe checking" is not optional.

Technology thinks long-term People are easily swayed towards short-term gratification at the expense of our happiness.

Bad technology exploits this for its own benefit. Good technology nudges us to do what's best for us years and decades down the line.

Technology embraces intuition Humans expect things to move when they push them, are exceptional at remembering physical spaces, and have an inherent desire for social contact. With this knowledge, we can build things that go hand-in-hand with our instincts.
Technology is opinionated Everything we build reflects the opinion and ideals of its maker. Don't avoid thatâinstead, scrutinize your opinion so the technology reflects good ideas.

Technology is contained The tools, the gadgets, the innovationsâthey're just a means to an end. At some point, they have to step away.

Let the robots be more robotic so the humans can be more human.

If you see this on your camera, it's flickering.

Your nervous system doesn't like flickering.

Lots of offices use fluorescent lights.

They have a weird color and flicker.

Effects: Headaches, getting tired, bad mood

title: You're not "talking to AI"

caption:
I think many of us—including me—want to feel seen. Heard. Appreciated.

It’s not a “bad” thing: socially, it makes sense that we want to behave like a good citizen.

But it’s also a prison.

Too often, we hold back our love because maybe it won’t be reciprocated.

Too often, we don’t publish that work because nobody might see it. Because people might laugh at us, criticize us, take away our hopes and dreams.

Too often, we only realize at the end of our lives that…

Our real joy came from the *giving*, not from the praise.

Would you rather:

• Have given this world everything you have, have burned up completely in your offering to the universe, without anyone applauding; or
• Just have given a few small things to the world, but gotten praise and love for them?

But the real benefit from letting go of attachment to the outcome is in the present.

You can actually BE in the present.

Your freedom of expression is allowed to flow.

And yes, that comes with feeling bad.

But because you allow it, your creativity is allowed to bloom as well. You have more energy to create and to give. You laugh harder, you look people in the eyes more deeply, you dance with less constraint.

And that…is worth all the effort.

I’m learning.
I will for the rest of my life.
And I won’t give up.
